l2-1.title: All about that WOMEN
l2-1.shortIntro: Exploring how women interact and collaborate via AI/technology-aided communication in online communities.

details.fullDesc: I am a woman. I care about women. However, women are not adequately cared by the world. Therefore, as a women, I become an observer of the world from a women's perspective, and try to make more women be seen and heard by the world. In this thread of project, I explore how technology can help women become visible and empowered, also how the solidarity in the women community can be cultivated and strengthened, especially in the context of Chinese social media and online communities.

outcomes.title: From Platform Migration to Cultural Integration: the Ingress and Diffusion of #wlw from TikTok to RedNote in Queer Women Communities
outcomes.authors: Ziqi Pan, Runhua Zhang, Jiehui Luo, Yuanhao Zhang, Yue Deng, Xiaojuan Ma
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: How would a foreign hashtag find its home in Chinese queer women community?

outcomes.title: When LLMs Enter Everyday Feminism on Chinese Social Media: Opportunities and Risks for Women’s Empowerment
outcomes.authors: Runhua Zhang, Ziqi Pan, Qiaoyi Chen, Kangyu Yuan, Yulin Tian, Huamin Qu, Xiaojuan Ma
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: How LLM affect Chinese women's navigation to empowerment in online communities.

l2-1.title: Obeservation of the Interesting Netizens
l2-1.shortIntro: Exploring how women interact and collaborate via AI/technology-aided communication in online communities.

details.fullDesc: The world-wide-web is an umbrella that covers all kinds of people, and the internet is a mirror that reflects the diversity of human beings. In this thread of project, I observe and explore the interesting netizens in the world-wide-web, especially the sub-communities like online immigrants induced by platform regulations, and the fans who found parasocial relationships much easier to build and enjoy in the current era of AI and technology. By understanding their interactions and needs, I hope to design better technological support for them to interact with the world and with each other in a more empowered way.

outcomes.title: 'I Love the Internet Again': Exploring the Interaction Inception of 'TikTok Refugees' Flocking into RedNote
outcomes.authors: Kangyu Yuan, Li Zhang, Hanfang Lyu, Ziqi Pan, Yuanhao Zhang, Junze Li, Bingcan Guo, Jiaxiong Hu, Qingyu Guo, Xiaojuan Ma
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: What actually happened for TikTok Refugees.

l2-2.title: Human-Compatible Interactions in Real-World
l2-2.shortIntro: Exploring modeling and designing interactions with realworld objects that are compatible with human knowledge and values based on affordances.

details.fullDesc: Though virtual intelligence is booming, the physical world is still the main stage for human interactions. In this thread of project, I explore how to model and design interactions with objects in a way that compatible with human values and perceptions based on objects' affordances. By understanding how people perceive and interact with objects in the physical world, I hope to develop agents that could behave the same way as humans do and design new interactions approach with digital devices that are natural and empowering for people in their daily lives.

outcomes.title: ACKnowledge: A Computational Framework for Human Compatible Affordance-based Interaction Planning in Real-world Contexts
outcomes.authors: Ziqi Pan, Xiucheng Zhang, Zisu Li, Zhenhui Peng, Mingming Fan, Xiaojuan Ma
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: Trying to build the brain of Ironman's JARVIS.

outcomes.title: Squeez’in: Private authentication on smartphones based on squeezing gestures
outcomes.authors: Xin Yi, Shuning Zhang, Ziqi Pan, Louisa Shi, Fengyan Han, Yan Kong, Hewu Li, Yuanchun Shi
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: Unlock your phone by squeezing it as you like.

l2-2.title: Facilitating Human-Human Interaction in Real-World
l2-2.shortIntro: Exploring how robots or intelligent agents can facilitate and enhance human-human interaction.

details.fullDesc: Besides the interactions between human and objects, human-human interaction is also a fundamental part of human life in this physical world. In this thread of project, I explore how to design and develop robots or intelligent agents that can facilitate and enhance human-human interaction, especially in face-to-face scenarios. By understanding the dynamics of human-human interaction and the potential role of technology in it, I hope to integrate technology seamlessly into human social contexts and promote more effective and enjoyable interactions among people.

outcomes.title: Moving Phones, Active Peers: Exploring the Effect of Animated Phones as Facilitators in In-Person Group Discussion
outcomes.authors: Ziqi Pan, Ziqi Liu, Jinhan Zhang, Zeyu Huang, Xiaojuan Ma
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: What if your phone can actively move to facilitate you to collaborate with your group mates?

l2-2.title: Human-Robot Teaming
l2-2.shortIntro: Exploring how robots or intelligent agents can collaborate with humans in common human-human interaction scenarios.

details.fullDesc: Beyond assistant or facilitator, technology can also be a teammate that collaborates with humans in common human-human interaction scenarios. In this thread of project, I explore how robots or intelligent agents may collaborate with humans, especially in teamwork scenarios. Based on the understanding of human-human interaction, I hope to explore (1) what kind of role and function a robot or intelligent agent may uniquely play in a human-robot team and how such roles may influence human perception and behavior, and (2) how to align the way to make contributions in group work between the robot or intelligent agent and the human teammates, so that the robot or intelligent agent can be a more compatible and effective teammate for humans in their daily life and work.

outcomes.title: Friend, Foe, or Bot? Exploring Intergroup Dynamics in Hybrid Human-Bot Teams
outcomes.authors: Assem Zhunis, Ziqi Pan, Yuanhao Zhang, Xiaojuan Ma
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: How bots participation affect the perception and behavior of humans in teamwork.

outcomes.title: Computational Approaches to Cognition Alignment in Human-Robot Teaming for Improved Collaborative Performance: A Survey
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: How to align the cognition of human and robot in Human-Robot teams.

l2-3.title: Human-Self Interaction through Physical Experience
l2-3.shortIntro: Exploring how human can interact with themselves with the help of physical intelligent companions.

details.fullDesc: This is a direction currently in its early stage and I am still exploring the research questions and design space. I am driven to explore how technology can help humans interact with themselves because of myself. I have experienced depression and anxiety at the beginning of my PhD journey and I am still trying to cope with them in the best way. During my fight against those emotions, I found that the interactions with myself are crucial. However, I am not always in a good condition to interact with myself in a positive way. Therefore, I am interested in exploring how technology can help facilitate human-self interaction, especially through physical experience with embodied and physical interfaces such as robots and interactive objects.

outcomes.title: Not yet decided: helper for human-self interaction for stress management
outcomes.authors: Ziqi Pan, xxx (Callling for Collaboration), Xiaojuan Ma
outcomes.intro: TL;DR: The best is yet to come ???

l2-5.title: Maybe I've debuted as an Acapella Musician?
l2-5.shortIntro: I am blessed to have more than 10k people hear my voice as aca singer.

details.story:
我总说，总会因为拿起麦克风而心动，无论是主持还是唱歌。

I always say that I can’t help falling in love the moment I pick up a microphone, whether I’m hosting or singing.

其实我不算是一个有唱歌天赋的人。小时候因为特别自信所以报名了一个很有名气的合唱团，然而，去到合唱团的第一次入团测试，老师就跟我说，‘‘要么你再练一周，要么你就别来了’’。我的父母对我，比我对我自己还要自信，听到这样的话自然不开心，二话没说就打算劝我，别唱了。而向来对父母言听计从的乖乖小孩，我，却第一次做出了和爸妈相悖的选择，‘‘帮我找个学音乐的大学生吧，我想再试试’’。

Strictly speaking, I’m not someone with a natural gift for singing. When I was little, I was very confident and signed up for a well‑known choir. But at the very first audition, the teacher told me, “Either practice for another week, or don’t come back.” My parents had even more faith in me than I had in myself, so they were understandably upset to hear that, and immediately tried to persuade me to quit singing. I had always been the obedient child who did whatever my parents said, but for the first time I made a choice that went against them: “Please help me find a college student who studies music. I want to give it another try.”

那次的坚持或许出于自尊或者倔强，但它的结果，是让我爱上了唱歌，尤其是合唱。我喜欢和声的原因，正是因为就算像我这样没什么声乐天赋小孩，也能因为练习努力音准准确而成为和声中很重要的人；就算像我这样没有什么声音特色的小孩，也能因为没有什么特色而能够创造出最和谐的声音。在和声里，我感受到了世界里很难触及的对不完美的包容，感受到了不完美的自己也很重要。

Maybe that persistence came from pride or stubbornness, but the result was that I fell in love with singing, especially choral music. The reason I love harmony is that even a kid like me, without much vocal talent, can become an important part of the sound by working hard and singing in tune; and even a kid like me, whose voice has no particular “character,” can precisely because of that blend in and help create the most harmonious sound. In harmony, I felt a rare kind of acceptance of imperfection, and I felt that my imperfect self could also matter.

后来，我开始接触到比起传统合唱更有可塑性也更具难度的纯人声无伴奏合唱（Acapella），并且很彻底地爱上了她。在阿卡的世界里，比起合唱，我又发现了自己更多的可能性，我可以去模仿乐器，可以去尝试改变声线。如果说合唱教会了我，普普通通也可以是一种竞争力，那么阿卡贝拉则告诉了我，没有特色的声音可以通过努力和雕琢变得具有更强的可塑性，正如璞玉比起美玉更有打磨的价值。

Later on, I was introduced to a form that is even more flexible and challenging than traditional choir: pure a cappella, and I fell head over heels in love with it. In the world of a cappella, compared with choir, I discovered even more possibilities for myself: I could imitate instruments, and I could experiment with changing my vocal tone. If choir taught me that being ordinary can itself be a kind of strength, then a cappella taught me that a voice without distinctive features can, through hard work and refinement, become highly malleable—like how an unpolished stone has more room to be carved than a finished gem.

我的喜爱很幸运地与我拥有的机遇重合了，无论是在清华、牛津还是港科，我都遇到了愿意让我成为一员的阿卡团，Maj9、OXCAR、Tunatics。他们成为我失落时的温柔乡，孤独时回声，以及找不到自己的意义时认可我的奖章。我在一次次演出和排练中，我从大家需要带着我练的小孩，变成可以办成一场演出的成员，而今又成长为可以被年轻朋友们依靠的music director。如今，可能已经有超过一万个人听过我的歌声了，我在礼堂、街头、体育馆、音乐厅的舞台上热烈地活着，第无数次为阿卡贝拉心动。谢谢大家和我一起唱歌，谢谢大家听我们唱歌，还要继续做很多舞台，编很多曲，唱很多歌。

I’ve been lucky that my passion has overlapped with the opportunities I’ve had. At Tsinghua, Oxford, and HKUST, I’ve all found a cappella groups willing to take me in: Maj9, OXCAR, Tunatics. They became my haven when I was frustrated, my echo when I was lonely, and my medal of recognition when I couldn’t find meaning in myself. Through round after round of rehearsals and performances, I went from being the kid everyone had to guide along, to a member who could help put on a full show, and now to a music director whom younger friends can rely on. By now, maybe more than ten thousand people have heard me sing. On stages in auditoriums, on the street, in sports arenas, and in concert halls, I’ve been living fiercely and vividly, falling in love with a cappella for the countless time. Thank you for singing with me, thank you for listening to us sing. I still want to stand on many more stages, arrange many more songs, and sing many, many more.
